title: oxytropis campestris var. cervinus
intro: Oxytropis campestris var. cervinus, also known as the mountain milkvetch, is a variant of the Oxytropis campestris plant, a perennial legume native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its alkaloid and flavonoid content. The mountain milkvetch is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat various ailments in traditional medicine.
